title: Create date variable for MockData — create_date_var
text: var character. Variable name generate (column name output) databaseStart character. Database/cycle identifier filtering metadata (e.g., "cchs2001_p", "minimal-example"). Used filter variables variable_details specified database. variables data.frame character. Variable-level metadata containing: variable: Variable names database: Database identifier (optional, filtering) sourceFormat: Output format ("analysis"/"csv"/"sas") distribution: Distribution type (uniform/gompertz/exponential) followup_min, followup_max: Followup period parameters event_prop: Proportion experiencing event garbage_high_prop, garbage_high_range: Garbage data parameters Can also file path (character) variables.csv. variable_details data.frame character. Detail-level metadata containing: variable: Variable name (joining) recStart: Date range (e.g., [01JAN2001,31DEC2020]) followup period recEnd: Classification (copy, NA::, NA::b) proportion: Category proportion missing codes Can also file path (character) variable_details.csv. df_mock data.frame. Optional. Existing mock data (check variable already exists). survival variables, may contain anchor_date column computing event dates. prop_missing numeric. Proportion missing values (0-1). Default 0 (missing). n integer. Number observations generate. seed integer. Optional. Random seed reproducibility.

title: Create date variable for MockData — create_date_var
text: data.frame one column (generated date variable), NULL : Variable found metadata Variable already exists df_mock valid date range found variable_details

title: Create date variable for MockData — create_date_var
text: v0.3.0 API: function now accepts full metadata data frames filters internally specified variable database. "recodeflow pattern" filtering handled inside function. Generation process: Filter metadata: Extract rows specified var + database Extract date parameters: Read variables.csv variable_details Generate population: Based distribution type (uniform/gompertz/exponential) Apply missing codes: proportions specified metadata Apply garbage: Read garbage parameters variables.csv Apply sourceFormat: Convert specified format (analysis/csv/sas) Output format (sourceFormat): sourceFormat column variables.csv controls output data type: "analysis": R Date objects (default) "csv": Character ISO strings (e.g., "2001-01-15") "sas": Numeric days since 1960-01-01 Distribution types: "uniform": Uniform distribution date range "gompertz": Gompertz survival distribution (time--event data) "exponential": Exponential distribution (events concentrated near start) Survival data generation: variables followup_min/followup_max/event_prop variables.csv: Requires anchor_date column df_mock (cohort entry/baseline date) Generates event times within followup window event_prop controls proportion experiencing event (vs. censored) Distribution controls event timing (Gompertz typical survival) Missing data: Missing codes identified recEnd containing "NA::": NA::: Skip codes (applicable) NA::b: Missing codes (know, refusal, stated) Garbage data: Garbage parameters read variables.csv: garbage_high_prop, garbage_high_range: Future dates (temporal violations)

title: Create date variable for MockData — create_date_var

what: Create mock data from configuration files — create_mock_data
title: Create mock data from configuration files — create_mock_data
text: Main orchestrator function generates complete mock datasets configuration files. Reads metadata, filters enabled variables, dispatches type-specific create_* functions, assembles results complete data frame.

title: Create mock data from configuration files — create_mock_data
code: create_mock_data(   databaseStart,   variables,   variable_details = NULL,   n = 1000,   seed = NULL,   validate = TRUE,   verbose = FALSE )

title: Create mock data from configuration files — create_mock_data
text: databaseStart Character. database identifier (e.g., "cchs2001_p", "minimal-example"). Used filter variables available specified database. variables data.frame character. Variable-level metadata containing: variable: Variable names variableType: Variable type (Categorical/Continuous/Date) role: Role tags (enabled, predictor, outcome, etc.) position: Display order (optional) database: Database filter (optional) Can also file path (character) variables.csv. variable_details data.frame character. Detail-level metadata containing: variable: Variable name (joining) recStart: Category code/range date interval recEnd: Classification (numeric code, "NA::", "NA::b") proportion: Category proportion (categorical) catLabel: Category label/description Can also file path (character) variable_details.csv. NULL, uses fallback mode (uniform distributions). n Integer. Number observations generate (default 1000). seed Integer. Optional random seed reproducibility. validate Logical. Whether validate configuration files (default TRUE). verbose Logical. Whether print progress messages (default FALSE).

title: Create mock data from configuration files — create_mock_data
text: Data frame n rows one column per enabled variable.

title: Create mock data from configuration files — create_mock_data
text: v0.3.0 API: function now follows "recodeflow pattern" passes full metadata data frames create_* functions, handle internal filtering. Generation process: Load metadata file paths accept data frames Filter enabled variables (role contains "enabled") Set global seed (provided) Loop variables position order: - Dispatch create_cat_var, create_con_var, create_date_var - Pass full metadata data frames (functions filter internally) - Merge result data frame Return complete dataset Fallback mode: variable_details = NULL, uses uniform distributions enabled variables. Variable types supported: Categorical: create_cat_var() Continuous: create_con_var() Date: create_date_var() Configuration schema: complete documentation configuration columns, see vignette("reference-config", package = "MockData").

title: Create mock data from configuration files — create_mock_data
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # Basic usage with file paths mock_data <- create_mock_data(   databaseStart = "minimal-example",   variables = "inst/extdata/minimal-example/variables.csv",   variable_details = "inst/extdata/minimal-example/variable_details.csv",   n = 1000,   seed = 123 )  # With data frames instead of file paths variables <- read.csv("inst/extdata/minimal-example/variables.csv",                       stringsAsFactors = FALSE) variable_details <- read.csv("inst/extdata/minimal-example/variable_details.csv",                               stringsAsFactors = FALSE)  mock_data <- create_mock_data(   databaseStart = "minimal-example",   variables = variables,   variable_details = variable_details,   n = 1000,   seed = 123 )  # Fallback mode (uniform distributions, no variable_details) mock_data <- create_mock_data(   databaseStart = "minimal-example",   variables = "inst/extdata/minimal-example/variables.csv",   variable_details = NULL,   n = 500 )  # View structure str(mock_data) head(mock_data) } # }

what: Create paired survival dates for cohort studies — create_survival_dates
title: Create paired survival dates for cohort studies — create_survival_dates
text: Generates entry event dates guaranteed temporal ordering (entry < event). Useful survival analysis, cohort studies, time--event modeling.

title: Create paired survival dates for cohort studies — create_survival_dates
code: create_survival_dates(   entry_var_row = NULL,   entry_details_subset = NULL,   event_var_row = NULL,   event_details_subset = NULL,   n = NULL,   seed = NULL,   df_mock = NULL,   entry_var = NULL,   event_var = NULL,   entry_start = NULL,   entry_end = NULL,   followup_min = NULL,   followup_max = NULL,   length = NULL,   event_distribution = "uniform",   prop_censored = 0,   prop_NA = NULL,   prop_invalid = NULL )

title: Create paired survival dates for cohort studies — create_survival_dates
text: entry_var_row data.frame. Single row mock_data_config entry date variable entry_details_subset data.frame. Rows mock_data_config_details entry date event_var_row data.frame. Single row mock_data_config event date variable event_details_subset data.frame. Rows mock_data_config_details event date n integer. Number observations generate seed integer. Random seed reproducibility. NULL, uses global seed. df_mock data.frame. current mock data (check variables already exist) Configuration v0.1 format (LEGACY): entry_var character. Name entry date variable event_var character. Name event date variable entry_start Date. Start entry period entry_end Date. End entry period followup_min integer. Minimum follow-days followup_max integer. Maximum follow-days length integer. Number records generate event_distribution character. Distribution time--event: "uniform", "gompertz", "exponential" prop_censored numeric. Proportion records censor (0-1) prop_NA numeric. Proportion missing values (0-1) prop_invalid numeric. Optional. Proportion temporal violations (entry > event) (0 1). NULL, invalid dates generated.

title: Create paired survival dates for cohort studies — create_survival_dates
text: data.frame entry_date, event_date, optionally event_status columns, NULL : Variables already exist df_mock Missing required configuration

title: Create paired survival dates for cohort studies — create_survival_dates
text: Configuration v0.2 format (NEW): v0.2 format (NEW): Extracts date ranges entry_details_subset event_details_subset Generates entry dates uniformly distributed Calculates event dates ensure entry < event Supports garbage data via catLabel::garbage event_details_subset Supports fallback mode: reasonable defaults details_subset NULL v0.1 format (LEGACY): Accepts explicit date ranges follow-parameters Supports multiple event distributions (uniform, gompertz, exponential) Handles censoring, missing values, temporal violations via parameters function auto-detects format based parameter names. function generates realistic survival data : Creating entry dates uniformly distributed across entry period Generating follow-times using specified distribution Calculating event dates (entry + follow-) Optionally censoring events (event_status = 0) Ensuring entry_date < event_date records Event distributions: "uniform": Constant hazard follow-period "gompertz": Increasing hazard (mortality increases time) "exponential": Decreasing hazard (early events common) Censoring: prop_censored > 0, generates event_status column: 1 = event observed 0 = censored (event_date becomes censoring date)

title: Create paired survival dates for cohort studies — create_survival_dates
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # v0.2 format - called by create_mock_data() config <- read_mock_data_config("mock_data_config.csv") details <- read_mock_data_config_details("mock_data_config_details.csv") entry_row <- config[config$variable == "study_entry", ] entry_details <- get_variable_details(details, variable_name = "study_entry") event_row <- config[config$variable == "death_date", ] event_details <- get_variable_details(details, variable_name = "death_date") surv_data <- create_survival_dates(   entry_var_row = entry_row,   entry_details_subset = entry_details,   event_var_row = event_row,   event_details_subset = event_details,   n = 1000,   seed = 123 )  # v0.2 with garbage (catLabel::garbage in config_details) # In mock_data_config_details.csv: # variable,recEnd,catLabel,proportion # death_date,followup_min,, # death_date,followup_max,, # death_date,garbage,garbage,0.03 event_details_with_garbage <- get_variable_details(details, variable_name = "death_date") surv_data_garbage <- create_survival_dates(   entry_var_row = entry_row,   entry_details_subset = entry_details,   event_var_row = event_row,   event_details_subset = event_details_with_garbage,   n = 1000,   seed = 123 )  # v0.1 format (legacy) - Basic mortality study surv_data <- create_survival_dates(   entry_var = "study_entry",   event_var = "death_date",   entry_start = as.Date("2000-01-01"),   entry_end = as.Date("2005-12-31"),   followup_min = 365,   followup_max = 3650,   length = 1000,   df_mock = data.frame(),   event_distribution = "gompertz" )  # v0.1 with censoring surv_data <- create_survival_dates(   entry_var = "cohort_entry",   event_var = "event_date",   entry_start = as.Date("2010-01-01"),   entry_end = as.Date("2015-12-31"),   followup_min = 30,   followup_max = 1825,   length = 500,   df_mock = data.frame(),   event_distribution = "exponential",   prop_censored = 0.3 )  # v0.1 with temporal violations for validation testing surv_data <- create_survival_dates(   entry_var = "interview_date",   event_var = "death_date",   entry_start = as.Date("2015-01-01"),   entry_end = as.Date("2016-12-31"),   followup_min = 30,   followup_max = 3650,   length = 1000,   df_mock = data.frame(),   prop_invalid = 0.03  # 3% temporal violations ) } # }

what: Create wide survival data for cohort studies — create_wide_survival_data
title: Create wide survival data for cohort studies — create_wide_survival_data
text: Generates wide-format survival data (one row per individual) 5 date variables (entry, event, death, loss--follow-, administrative censoring). Applies temporal ordering constraints supports garbage data generation QA testing.

title: Create wide survival data for cohort studies — create_wide_survival_data
code: create_wide_survival_data(   var_entry_date,   var_event_date = NULL,   var_death_date = NULL,   var_ltfu = NULL,   var_admin_censor = NULL,   databaseStart,   variables,   variable_details,   df_mock = NULL,   n,   seed = NULL,   prop_garbage = NULL )

title: Create wide survival data for cohort studies — create_wide_survival_data
text: var_entry_date character. Required. Variable name entry date (baseline). var_event_date character. Optional. Variable name primary event date (e.g., "dementia_incid_date"). Set NULL skip. var_death_date character. Optional. Variable name death date (competing risk). Set NULL skip. var_ltfu character. Optional. Variable name loss--follow-date. Set NULL skip. var_admin_censor character. Optional. Variable name administrative censoring date. Set NULL skip. databaseStart character. Required. Database identifier filtering metadata (used databaseStart column variable_details). variables data.frame. Required. Full variables metadata (pre-filtered). Must contain columns: variable, variableType. variable_details data.frame. Required. Full variable details metadata (pre-filtered). filtered internally using databaseStart column. df_mock data.frame. Optional. current mock data check variables already exist use anchor_date source. Default: NULL. n integer. Required. Number observations generate. seed integer. Optional. Random seed reproducibility. Default: NULL. prop_garbage numeric. DEPRECATED v0.3.1. parameter longer supported. generate temporal violations QA testing, use garbage_high_prop garbage_high_range parameters variables.csv individual date variables. See Details section migration guidance. Default: NULL.

title: Create wide survival data for cohort studies — create_wide_survival_data
text: data.frame 1-5 date columns (depending variables specified), NULL variables already exist df_mock.

title: Create wide survival data for cohort studies — create_wide_survival_data
text: function implements v0.3.0 "recodeflow pattern" API: Accepts full metadata data frames (pre-filtered subsets) Accepts variable names (variable rows) Filters metadata internally using databaseStart column Implementation strategy: Call create_date_var() non-NULL date variable variable configured separately variables.csv event_prop, distribution, followup_min, followup_max, etc. Combine results single data frame Apply temporal ordering constraints Temporal ordering constraints (normal mode): Entry date always baseline (earliest) dates must >= entry_date Death can occur event death < event, set event NA (censored, missing) Observation ends min(event, death, ltfu, admin_censor) Temporal violations QA testing (v0.3.1+): function creates clean, temporally-ordered survival data. generate temporal violations testing data quality pipelines: Add garbage_high_prop garbage_high_range individual date variables variables.csv (e.g., future death dates beyond 2025) Use create_date_var() generate date variables garbage Test temporal validation logic separately approach separates concerns: date-level garbage vs. survival data generation Migration prop_garbage (deprecated v0.3.1):   Configuration metadata: date variable must defined variables.csv variable_details.csv: variables.csv:   variable_details.csv:
code: # OLD (v0.3.0): surv <- create_wide_survival_data(..., prop_garbage = 0.03)  # NEW (v0.3.1+): # Add garbage to date variables in metadata variables$garbage_high_prop[variables$variable == "death_date"] <- 0.03 variables$garbage_high_range[variables$variable == "death_date"] <-   "[2025-01-01, 2099-12-31]" # create_date_var() will apply garbage automatically surv <- create_wide_survival_data(..., variables = variables) variable,variableType,role interview_date,Date,enabled dementia_incid_date,Date,enabled death_date,Date,enabled variable,recStart,recEnd,value,proportion interview_date,[2001-01-01,2005-12-31],copy,NA,NA dementia_incid_date,[2002-01-01,2021-01-01],followup_min,365,NA dementia_incid_date,NA,followup_max,7300,NA dementia_incid_date,NA,event_prop,0.15,NA death_date,[2002-01-01,2026-01-01],followup_min,365,NA death_date,NA,followup_max,9125,NA death_date,NA,event_prop,0.40,NA

title: Create wide survival data for cohort studies — create_wide_survival_data
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # Read metadata variables <- read.csv("inst/extdata/survival/variables.csv") variable_details <- read.csv("inst/extdata/survival/variable_details.csv")  # Generate 5-variable survival data surv_data <- create_wide_survival_data(   var_entry_date = "interview_date",   var_event_date = "dementia_incid_date",   var_death_date = "death_date",   var_ltfu = "ltfu_date",   var_admin_censor = "admin_censor_date",   databaseStart = "demport",   variables = variables,   variable_details = variable_details,   n = 1000,   seed = 123 )  # Generate minimal survival data (entry + event only) surv_data <- create_wide_survival_data(   var_entry_date = "cohort_entry",   var_event_date = "primary_event_date",   var_death_date = NULL,   var_ltfu = NULL,   var_admin_censor = NULL,   database = "study",   variables = variables,   variable_details = variable_details,   n = 500,   seed = 456 )  # Generate with garbage data for QA testing (v0.3.1+) # Add garbage to death_date in metadata vars_with_garbage <- add_garbage(variables, "death_date",   high_prop = 0.05, high_range = "[2025-01-01, 2099-12-31]")  surv_data <- create_wide_survival_data(   var_entry_date = "interview_date",   var_event_date = "dementia_incid_date",   var_death_date = "death_date",   var_ltfu = NULL,   var_admin_censor = NULL,   databaseStart = "demport",   variables = vars_with_garbage,  # Use modified metadata   variable_details = variable_details,   n = 1000,   seed = 789 ) } # }

text: Parses proportion column organizes type (valid, missing, garbage). Validates valid + missing proportions sum 1.0 (±0.001 tolerance). Auto-normalizes warning sum != 1.0.

title: Extract proportions from details subset — extract_proportions
text: details_subset Data frame. Rows details one variable. variable_name Character. Variable name error messages.

title: Extract proportions from details subset — extract_proportions
text: Named list : valid: Numeric. Proportion valid values (sum non-missing, non-garbage) missing: Named list. Proportion missing code (e.g., "7" = 0.03) garbage: Named list. Proportion garbage type (e.g., garbage_low = 0.02) categories: Character vector. non-garbage recStart values category_proportions: Numeric vector. Proportions sampling (aligned categories)

title: Extract proportions from details subset — extract_proportions
text: Population proportions (valid + missing) must sum 1.0. Garbage proportions separate applied valid values . proportions NA missing, returns uniform probabilities.

Derived variables ("DerivedVar::" variableStart) return NA variable_raw since require custom derivation logic.

what: Identify derived variables using recodeflow patterns — identify_derived_vars
title: Identify derived variables using recodeflow patterns — identify_derived_vars
text: Identifies derived variables using recodeflow metadata patterns variable_details. Derived variables calculated raw variables (e.g., BMI height/weight) generated mock data. function uses recodeflow approach: derived variables identified metadata patterns variable_details, role column flags.

title: Identify derived variables using recodeflow patterns — identify_derived_vars
code: identify_derived_vars(variables, variable_details)

title: Identify derived variables using recodeflow patterns — identify_derived_vars
text: variables data.frame. Variable-level metadata column: variable variable_details data.frame. Required. Detail-level metadata columns: variable, recStart, recEnd. Contains DerivedVar:: Func:: patterns identify derived variables.

title: Identify derived variables using recodeflow patterns — identify_derived_vars
text: Character vector derived variable names (may empty none found)

title: Identify derived variables using recodeflow patterns — identify_derived_vars
text: Detection methods (recodeflow patterns ): DerivedVar:: pattern: Variables DerivedVar:: recStart column Example: recStart = "DerivedVar::[HWTGHTM, HWTGWTK]" Indicates variable derived listed raw variables Func:: pattern: Variables Func:: recEnd column Example: recEnd = "Func::bmi_fun" Indicates transformation function applied derive variable variable_details required: recodeflow approach stores derivation logic variable_details, variables. role column (present) used derived variable detection. ensures: Derivation logic explicit (variables, function) Metadata self-documenting Compatible cchsflow recodeflow tools Note: function check role = "derived" even present. Derived status determined solely DerivedVar:: Func:: patterns.

title: Identify derived variables using recodeflow patterns — identify_derived_vars
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # BMI derived from height and weight variables <- data.frame(   variable = c("height", "weight", "BMI_derived"),   variableType = c("Continuous", "Continuous", "Continuous"),   role = c("enabled", "enabled", "enabled"),  # NO "derived" flag needed   stringsAsFactors = FALSE )  variable_details <- data.frame(   variable = c("height", "weight", "BMI_derived"),   recStart = c("[1.4,2.1]", "[45,150]", "DerivedVar::[height, weight]"),   recEnd = c("copy", "copy", "Func::bmi_fun"),   stringsAsFactors = FALSE )  identify_derived_vars(variables, variable_details) # Returns: "BMI_derived"  # ADL derived from 5 ADL items variable_details_adl <- data.frame(   variable = c("ADL_01", "ADL_02", "ADL_03", "ADL_04", "ADL_05", "ADL_der"),   recStart = c(rep("1", 5), "DerivedVar::[ADL_01, ADL_02, ADL_03, ADL_04, ADL_05]"),   recEnd = c(rep("1", 5), "Func::adl_fun"),   stringsAsFactors = FALSE )  identify_derived_vars(variables, variable_details_adl) # Returns: "ADL_der" } # }

title: Import and convert recodeflow variables and variable details metadata files to MockData configuration format — import_from_recodeflow
text: variables_path Character. Path recodeflow variables.csv file. variable_details_path Character. Path recodeflow variable_details.csv file. role_filter Character. Role value filter variables . variables role imported. Default "mockdata". Use regex word boundary matching avoid partial matches (e.g., "mockdata" match "mockdata_test"). database Character vector NULL. Database identifier(s) filter . NULL (default), extracts unique databases variables.csv databaseStart column. specified, imports variables exist specified database(s). output_dir Character. Directory output CSV files written. Default "inst/extdata/". Files named mock_data_config.csv mock_data_config_details.csv.

title: Import and convert recodeflow variables and variable details metadata files to MockData configuration format — import_from_recodeflow
text: Invisible list two data frames: config details

title: Import and convert recodeflow variables and variable details metadata files to MockData configuration format — import_from_recodeflow
text: Direct copy: variable, role, label, labelLong, section, subject, variableType, units, version, description (notes) Generated: uid: v_001, v_002, v_003, ... position: 10, 20, 30, ... source_database: extracted databaseStart based database filter source_spec: basename variables_path last_updated: current date mockDataLastUpdated: current date seed: NA rType: NA (user must fill : integer/double/factor/date/logical/character) garbage_low_prop, garbage_low_range, garbage_high_prop, garbage_high_range: NA mockDataVersion, mockDataVersionNotes: NA

title: Import and convert recodeflow variables and variable details metadata files to MockData configuration format — import_from_recodeflow
text: Direct copy: variable, dummyVariable, catStartLabel (catLabel), catLabelLong, units, notes Mapped: recStart: copied input recStart recEnd: initialized input recStart (user updates : valid, distribution, mean, etc.) Generated: uid: looked config variable name uid_detail: d_001, d_002, d_003, ... proportion: NA (user fills - must sum 1.0 per variable) value: NA (user fills distribution parameters) sourceFormat: NA (user fills date variables)

title: Make garbage — make_garbage

title: Make garbage — make_garbage
code: make_garbage(values, details_subset, variable_type, seed = NULL)

title: Make garbage — make_garbage
text: values Vector. Generated values (already valid + missing). details_subset Data frame. Rows details (contains garbage_* rows). variable_type Character. "categorical", "continuous", "date", "survival". seed Integer. Optional random seed.

title: Make garbage — make_garbage
text: Vector garbage applied.

title: Make garbage — make_garbage
text: Two-step garbage model: Identify valid value indices (missing codes) Sample valid indices based garbage proportions Replace garbage values Ensure overlap (use setdiff sequential garbage application) Garbage types: garbage_low: Values valid range (continuous, integer) garbage_high: Values valid range (continuous, integer) garbage_future: Future dates (date, survival)

title: Make garbage — make_garbage

what: Parse range notation from variable_details — parse_range_notation
title: Parse range notation from variable_details — parse_range_notation
text: Parses recodeflow-standard range notation strings variable_details.csv (recodes column) structured data mock data generation. Supports integer ranges, continuous ranges, special codes, function calls.

title: Parse range notation from variable_details — parse_range_notation
code: parse_range_notation(range_string, range_type = "auto", expand_integers = TRUE)

title: Parse range notation from variable_details — parse_range_notation
text: range_string Character string containing range notation range_type Character. One : "auto" (default): Auto-detect based bracket notation decimal values "integer": Force integer range interpretation (generates sequence) "continuous": Force continuous range interpretation expand_integers Logical. TRUE range_type "integer", returns integers range vector

title: Parse range notation from variable_details — parse_range_notation
text: continuous ranges: List min, max, min_inclusive, max_inclusive integer ranges: List min, max, values (expand_integers=TRUE) Returns NULL parsing fails

title: Parse range notation from variable_details — parse_range_notation
text: Range Notation Formats: Supports comma (recommended) semicolon (compatibility) delimiters: Integer ranges: [7,9] [7;9] → integers 7,8,9 Continuous ranges: [18.5,25) [18.5;25) → 18.5 ≤ x < 25 Continuous ranges: [18.5,25] [18.5;25] → 18.5 ≤ x ≤ 25 Infinity ranges: [30,inf) [30;inf) → x ≥ 30 Date ranges: [2001-01-01;2005-12-31] (semicolon recommended dates) Special codes: NA::, NA::b, copy, else (passed unchanged) Function calls: Func::function_name (passed unchanged) Mathematical Bracket Notation: [,b] [;b] - Closed interval: ≤ x ≤ b [,b) [;b) - Half-open interval: ≤ x < b (,b] (;b] - Half-open interval: < x ≤ b (,b) (;b) - Open interval: < x < b Auto-Detection Logic: Contains decimal values → continuous range Uses mathematical bracket notation [,b) → continuous range Simple [integer,integer] → integer range (generates sequence) Contains "inf" → continuous range [;b)` - Half-open interval: ≤ x < b `(,b]: R:;b)%60%20-%20Half-open%20interval:%20a%20%E2%89%A4%20x%20%3C%20b%0A-%20%60(,b [integer,integer]: R:integer,integer

title: Parse range notation from variable_details — parse_range_notation
text: Adapted cchsflow v4.0.0 (2025-07-27) - universal across recodeflow projects

title: Parse range notation from variable_details — parse_range_notation
code: # Integer ranges parse_range_notation("[7,9]") #> $type #> [1] "integer" #>  #> $min #> [1] 7 #>  #> $max #> [1] 9 #>  #> $values #> [1] 7 8 9 #>  #> $min_inclusive #> [1] TRUE #>  #> $max_inclusive #> [1] TRUE #>  # Returns: list(min=7, max=9, values=c(7,8,9), type="integer")  # Continuous ranges parse_range_notation("[18.5,25)") #> $type #> [1] "continuous" #>  #> $min #> [1] 18.5 #>  #> $max #> [1] 25 #>  #> $min_inclusive #> [1] TRUE #>  #> $max_inclusive #> [1] FALSE #>  # Returns: list(min=18.5, max=25, min_inclusive=TRUE, max_inclusive=FALSE, type="continuous")  parse_range_notation("[30,inf)") #> $type #> [1] "continuous" #>  #> $min #> [1] 30 #>  #> $max #> [1] Inf #>  #> $min_inclusive #> [1] TRUE #>  #> $max_inclusive #> [1] FALSE #>  # Returns: list(min=30, max=Inf, min_inclusive=TRUE, max_inclusive=FALSE, type="continuous")  # Special cases parse_range_notation("NA::a")   # Returns: list(type="special", value="NA::a") #> $type #> [1] "special" #>  #> $value #> [1] "NA::a" #>  parse_range_notation("copy")    # Returns: list(type="special", value="copy") #> $type #> [1] "special" #>  #> $value #> [1] "copy" #>  parse_range_notation("else")    # Returns: list(type="special", value="else") #> $type #> [1] "special" #>  #> $value #> [1] "else" #>

text: Character string raw variable name, NULL found.

title: Parse variableStart field to extract raw variable name — parse_variable_start
text: function implements recodeflow-standard parsing strategies: Database-prefixed format: "database::varname" - database-specific names Bracket format (whole string): "[varname]" - database-agnostic names Bracket format (segment): "database1::var1, [var2]" - [var2] DEFAULT databases Plain format: "varname" - uses value -Important: [variable] represents DEFAULT databases explicitly referenced database:: notation. reduces repetition one databases use different variable names. DerivedVar format, returns NULL (requires custom derivation logic).

title: Parse variableStart field to extract raw variable name — parse_variable_start
code: # Database-prefixed format parse_variable_start("cycle1::height, cycle2::HEIGHT", "cycle1") #> [1] "height" # Returns: "height"  # Bracket format (database-agnostic) parse_variable_start("[gen_015]", "cycle1") #> [1] "gen_015" # Returns: "gen_015"  # Mixed format - [variable] is DEFAULT for databases not explicitly listed parse_variable_start("cycle1::amsdmva1, [ammdmva1]", "cycle2") #> [1] "ammdmva1" # Returns: "ammdmva1" (uses default for cycle2)  # Plain format parse_variable_start("bmi", "cycle1") #> [1] "bmi" # Returns: "bmi"  # No match for specified database parse_variable_start("cycle2::age", "cycle1") #> NULL # Returns: NULL

text: Invisible NULL. Stops error message validation fails.

title: Validate MockData configuration details against schema requirements including proportion sums and parameter completeness — validate_mock_data_config_details
text: Validation checks: Required columns: variable, recStart (always required) recEnd (conditionally required using missing data codes 6-9, 96-99) uid, uid_detail (optional simple examples) Conditional recEnd requirement: recEnd column required recStart contains missing codes (6-9, 96-99) Enables classification: NA::(skip), NA::b (missing), numeric (valid) Without recEnd, missing vs. valid codes distinguished Uniqueness: uid_detail values must unique (column present) Proportion validation: Values must range [0, 1] Population proportions (valid + missing codes) must sum 1.0 ±0.001 per variable Garbage proportions (garbage_*) excluded sum Auto-normalizes warning sum ≠ 1.0 Parameter validation: Distribution-specific requirements: normal → mean + sd gompertz → rate + shape exponential → rate poisson → rate Link validation (config provided): uid values must exist config$uid Flexible recEnd validation: Warns error unknown recEnd values

title: Validate MockData configuration details against schema requirements including proportion sums and parameter completeness — validate_mock_data_config_details
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # Validate details details <- read.csv("mock_data_config_details.csv", stringsAsFactors = FALSE) validate_mock_data_config_details(details)  # Validate with cross-check against config config <- read.csv("mock_data_config.csv", stringsAsFactors = FALSE) validate_mock_data_config_details(details, config = config) } # }

title: MockData 0.3.0
text: New function API - generator functions now accept full metadata data frames instead pre-filtered subsets: Affected functions: create_cat_var(), create_con_var(), create_date_var(), create_wide_survival_data(), create_mock_data() Deprecated: prop_garbage parameter create_wide_survival_data(). Use garbage parameters metadata instead:
code: # Before (v0.2.x) var_row <- variables[variables$variable == "age", ] details_subset <- variable_details[variable_details$variable == "age", ] result <- create_con_var(var_row, details_subset, n = 1000)  # After (v0.3.0) result <- create_con_var(   var = "age",   databaseStart = "minimal-example",   variables = variables,   variable_details = variable_details,   n = 1000 ) # Old way (no longer supported) surv <- create_wide_survival_data(..., prop_garbage = 0.03)  # New way vars_with_garbage <- add_garbage(variables, "death_date",   garbage_high_prop = 0.03, garbage_high_range = "[2025-01-01, 2099-12-31]") surv <- create_wide_survival_data(..., variables = vars_with_garbage)

title: MockData 0.3.0
text: Unified garbage generation across variable types (categorical, continuous, date, survival): garbage_low_prop + garbage_low_range values valid range garbage_high_prop + garbage_high_range values valid range New helper function add_garbage() easy garbage specification Categorical garbage now supported (treats codes ordinal generate --range values) Derived variable identification: identify_derived_vars() - Identifies derived variables using DerivedVar:: Func:: patterns get_raw_var_dependencies() - Extracts raw variable dependencies Compatible recodeflow patterns
code: # Add garbage to any variable type vars_with_garbage <- add_garbage(variables, "smoking",   garbage_low_prop = 0.02, garbage_low_range = "[-2, 0]")  # Pipe-friendly vars_with_garbage <- variables %>%   add_garbage("age", garbage_high_prop = 0.03, garbage_high_range = "[150, 200]") %>%   add_garbage("smoking", garbage_low_prop = 0.02, garbage_low_range = "[-2, 0]")

title: MockData 0.3.0
text: Fixed categorical garbage factor level bug - garbage values converted NA factor creation Fixed recEnd column requirement - now optional simple configurations Fixed derived variable generation create_mock_data() - derived variables now correctly excluded

title: MockData 0.3.0
text: Restructured using Divio framework: Removed 6 vignettes (cchs-example, chms-example, demport-example, dates, schema-change-dates, tutorial-config-files) Added 2 new vignettes (tutorial-categorical-continuous, tutorial-survival-data) Massively expanded reference-config (2,028 lines comprehensive metadata schema documentation) vignettes updated v0.3.0 API examples now use inst/extdata/minimal-example/ Final structure (9 vignettes): Tutorials (6): getting-started, tutorial-categorical-continuous, tutorial-dates, tutorial-survival-data, tutorial-missing-data, tutorial-garbage-data -guides (1): -recodeflow-users Explanation (1): advanced-topics Reference (1): reference-config Metadata simplification: Removed inst/extdata/cchs/, inst/extdata/chms/, inst/extdata/demport/ inst/extdata/minimal-example/ remains canonical reference

title: MockData 0.3.0
text: Update function calls: Pass variable name string (pre-filtered row) Pass full metadata data frames (subsets) Add databaseStart parameter Remove manual filtering Update garbage specification: Remove prop_garbage create_wide_survival_data() calls Add garbage metadata using add_garbage() helper

title: MockData 0.2.0
text: Breaking change: New configuration schema uid/uid_detail system Replaces v0.1 cat/catLabel columns unified metadata structure Adds rType field explicit R type coercion (factor, integer, double, Date) Adds proportion field direct distribution control Adds date-specific fields: date_start, date_end, distribution Backward compatibility: v0.1 format still supported via dual interface. formats work side--side.

title: MockData 0.2.0
text: New create_date_var() function date variables Multiple distribution options: uniform, gompertz, exponential Support survival analysis patterns SAS date format parsing Three source formats: analysis (R Date), csv (ISO strings), sas (numeric)

title: MockData 0.2.0
text: New create_wide_survival_data() function cohort studies Generates paired entry event dates guaranteed temporal ordering Supports censoring multiple event distributions Note: Must called manually (compatible create_mock_data() batch generation)

title: MockData 0.2.0
text: New prop_invalid parameter across generators Generates intentionally invalid data testing validation pipelines Supports garbage types: corrupt_future, corrupt_past, corrupt_range Critical testing data cleaning workflows

title: MockData 0.2.0
text: New create_mock_data() function batch generation CSV configuration New read_mock_data_config() read_mock_data_config_details() readers Processes multiple variables single call Fallback mode details provided

text: Explicit rType field controls R type conversion Proper factor handling levels Integer vs double distinction age/count variables Makes generated data match real survey data types
